items.title: Hundreds arrested in violent clashes as fans celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League win
items.summary: Police have detained 780 people involved in violent clashes in Paris and other French cities that erupted after Paris Saint-Germain won the Champions League title.

items.title: Shirtless Aussie questioned over alleged cafe rampage in Vietnam
items.summary: An Australian tourist is being questioned by Vietnamese police after allegedly going on a rampage in a busy cafe.

items.title: Today in History - May 31: Conman exposed by what he ordered from the menu
items.summary: A long-time fraudster posing as a Saudi prince was jailed for 18 years on May 31, 2019.

items.title: Tech giant Microsoft unveiles $25 billion plan to reshape the Australian workplace
items.summary: A﻿nd the man in charge says Australia is in a prime position for the major growth technology.

items.title: Big hint Apple's first foldable smartphone is coming this year
items.summary: After years of rumours, it seems more likely than ever that Apple will introduce a foldable smartphone this year as part of a shake-up of its entire iPhone line-up this September.

items.title: Apple almost went broke – now it's celebrating 50 years
items.summary: H﻿ow the company came back from the brink to become a global juggernaut.

items.title: Sydney Opera House sails light up to mark 50 Years of Apple
items.summary: The sails of the Sydney Opera House will light up with colour over the next three nights as part of a global celebration of Apple's 50th birthday.

items.title: Aussies heading to the USA set for high-speed Wi-Fi in a travel revolution
items.summary: Australians heading to the United States are set for a big change in the way we travel, with United Airlines launching an all-new in-flight experience on their next-generation aircraft, including personalised infotainment and high-speed satellite internet.

items.title: Apple MacBook Neo review: The laptop market is redefined
items.summary: When the low-cost MacBook Neo was announced last week, I had to look twice to check the price was in Australian dollars and not US dollars because a sub-$1000 laptop from Apple was unimaginable, until now.

items.title: Bigfoot, trombone, orca among the latest emojis
items.summary: Have you ever wanted to send a text about orcas or Bigfoot or trombones only to find you have to do it using words instead of pictures?

items.title: Apple's latest iPhone is designed for those clinging onto older phones
items.summary: There are a lot of people using older model phones that are contemplating an upgrade. Could the newest version be the right fit?

items.title: Apple unveils $899 MacBook Neo in surprise launch
items.summary: It's a brand new model for the tech giant, and it's a big shift away from the typical price point.

items.title: Samsung's new phone feature blocks prying eyes
items.summary: Samsung's flagship smartphone for 2026 will launch with an industry-first privacy feature aimed at stopping those prying eyes around you from glimpsing what you're looking at on your screen.

items.title: New God of War game headlines 'epic' PlayStation State of Play event
items.summary: A new God of War game, exciting trailers, and several major reveals — PlayStation's February State of Play was one of the strongest showcases in recent memory.

items.title: Create your own Pokemon paradise with the latest in Nintendo life-sim
items.summary: After an hour rebuilding my own deserted island back from the brink, it's safe to say our favourite pocket monsters remain irresistibly charming.

items.title: Seven years on, Fallout 76 developers still listening to fans and now watching the TV series
items.summary: Seven years after its launch, video game Fallout 76 continues to evolve, with new updates shaped by both fan feedback and inspiration from Amazon's Fallout TV series, as they reveal to 9news.com.au.

items.title: The most anticipated video games of 2026
items.summary: 2026 is going to be see some incredible video games released - from the highly anticipated Grand Theft Auto VI to Xbox Game Studios' Fable.

items.title: Australian technology companies making it big on the world stage
items.summary: A﻿longside the tech giants at the CES trade show in Las Vegas are Australian start-ups displaying their products.

items.title: The TVs of tomorrow: Ultra-thin designs and 130-inch monsters
items.summary: CES, the biggest technology show in the world, is happening this week in Las Vegas, a place for the biggest brands and the newest startups to demonstrate their innovation to the world in the hope of making it into retail stores this year.

items.title: Apple issues urgent iPhone update after triple zero emergency call testing raises concerns
items.summary: Apple has released an emergency update to iOS 26 that applies only to the iPhone 12, addressing a compatibility issue with Australia's emergency calling system.

items.title: How Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora's From the Ashes expansion aligns with the new film
items.summary: Developed in collaboration between Massive Entertainment and Lightstorm Entertainment, the From the Ashes game expansion runs side by side to the events of Avatar: Fire and Ash.

items.title: Review: The best game on the Switch 2 yet
items.summary: It meets (and arguably exceeds) the exceptionally high bar set by its predecessors and is willing to gamble the franchise's small but loyal fanbase on a few big changes.

items.title: Everything we know about ChatGPT's 'erotica' rollout in December
items.summary: ChatGPT is about to get a lot hornier. Sam Altman, chief executive of the artificial intelligence chatbot's parent company, says it's about allowing adults more freedom to use AI how they want.

items.title: ‘The zombies have evolved’: Interview with Resident Evil Requiem producer
items.summary: Capcom, the developers of the classic Resident Evil franchise, are gearing up for their ninth major entry in the long running series — Resident Evil Requiem. 9news.com.au caught up with the game's producer.

items.title: 'Double-edged sword': How Aussie content creators are reacting to the under-16 social media ban
items.summary: As the under-16 social media ban approaches, Australian content creators have been left wondering how the restrictions will impact their careers, with some calling it a "double-edged sword."

items.title: REVIEW: As a long-lapsed Pokemon fan, it feels like Legends Z-A was made for me. But this is my only wish for the new game
items.summary: Now that the game is on store shelves, I'm happy to report that battles are still the best part of Pokemon Legends Z-A.

items.title: Australian cyber experts warn of the 'catastrophic' threat quantum computing could pose
items.summary: They have good reason to be nervous - the emergence of quantum computing looms as the biggest global security challenge in decades.

items.title: Xania Monet is the first AI-powered artist to debut on a Billboard airplay chart, but she likely won't be the last
items.summary: So far, Monet has appeared on multiple Billboard charts since first releasing a song in summer 2025, including the Hot Gospel Songs (for her song Let Go, Let God) and the Hot R&B Songs chart (for her song How Was I Supposed to Know), according to the publication.

items.title: Halo coming to PlayStation for the first time in new remake: 'Built from the ground up'
items.summary: Xbox and Halo Studios have officially announced a full remake of Halo: Combat Evolved, rebuilt from the ground up and launching on next year. 9news.com.au caught up with the creators.

items.title: Apple updates its premium iPad, MacBook Pro and even Vision Pro gets an upgrade
items.summary: This week three new products hit the stores - all of them year on year upgrades featuring Apple's newest processor, the M5 Apple Silicon.

items.title: More than 1600 customers of Dodo and iPrimus impacted by cybersecurity breach
items.summary: Another Aussie company has been hit by a cybersecurity breach, with more than 1600 customers of telco Dodo and iPrimus being impacted by a data breach.

items.title: Xbox game takes arachnophobia mode to the next level in new update
items.summary: The story of four teens shrunk down to bug size continues with Grounded 2, but this time the kids are trapped inside a huge park with new dangers and objectives. 9news.com.au caught up with the game's creator to learn all about it.

items.title: 'A dangerous game to play': Are we really in an AI tech bubble and what happens if it bursts?
items.summary: Even Sam Altman, the head of ChatGPT maker OpenAI, asked directly if investors were "overexcited about AI", flat out said "yes".

items.title: Google launching 'most powerful AI search experience yet' in Australia
items.summary: The search giant this morning announced it would be rolling out AI Mode, its "most powerful AI search experience yet", in Australia and more than 40 other countries this week.

items.title: More than 80,000 gamers to descend on Melbourne for PAX this week
items.summary: Tens of thousands of gamers across the country are packing their bags, ready to spawn in Melbourne for PAX Australia 2025 this week. Here's what to expect.

items.title: Baby Steps creators take us step by step through the game's development
items.summary: The video game world is currently buzzing about Baby Steps, the new release that's earning praise for being frustrating, awkward, and brilliant all at once. 9news.com.au met the team behind it.

items.title: Super Mario Galaxy and Super Mario Galaxy 2 bundle: Is it worth it?
items.summary: It's $89.95 on the Australian eShop. Outrageously, they're available separately for $59.95! That's a lot of money for any 15-year-old game, even ones as good as Super Mario Galaxy.

items.title: Sonic Team's Takashi Iizuka on Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds
items.summary: Sonic Team's Takashi Iizuka opens up about his move to Los Angeles to rebuild the Sonic name. Now, he's just released Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds, a high speed racing video game inviting characters from Microsoft, Nickelodeon and more.

items.title: LEGO Party offers fresh take on the classic Mario Party formula
items.summary: New Aussie made video game LEGO Party takes the classic Mario Party formula and reimagines it with a LEGO twist.

items.title: New game Ghost of Yōtei a ‘feast for the senses’
items.summary: In a year overflowing with excellent samurai and ninja titles, Ghost of Yōtei had a big job to do.

items.title: 'It's not the Pokemon you grew up playing': Pokemon Legends Z-A Preview
items.summary: A preview has never changed my opinion as quickly as Pokemon Legends Z-A. I went in apathetic and walked out hungry for more. This isn't the Pokemon I grew up playing.

items.title: Recall notice issued for some Tesla battery systems
items.summary: The ACCC issued a product recall of a Tesla battery after reports they can overheat and catch fire.

items.title: Apple say their new AirPods can translate in real time, does it actually work?
items.summary: Last week Apple revealed their new smartphones, the iPhone 17 lineup, but another reveal was their new headphones, the AirPods Pro 3.

items.title: Google will not be forced to sell off Chrome or Android, US judge rules in landmark antitrust ruling
items.summary: The decision overnight serves as a significant win for the tech company, which has avoided the most extreme possible outcome from a landmark antitrust case that determined the company was operating an illegal online search monopoly.

items.title: How home audio and headphones are assisting hearing impaired
items.summary: For years, hearing aids and high-end audio gear existed in separate worlds. But with the rise of AI that's all starting to change. Sonos' latest soundbar is bringing assistive hearing to the couch.

items.title: This game was already 'incredible'. It just got another reason to play
items.summary: It didn't need an expansion, but the fact it's getting one is all the more reason to dive back in.

items.title: Spotted a tiny black dot on your phone screen? It could be 'pixel cancer'
items.summary: It starts with a tiny black dot on your phone screen. Then, before you know it, your device is down with a bad case of 'pixel cancer'. Read more.

items.title: Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 revealed
items.summary: Activision has officially lifted the lid on Call of Duty: Black Ops 7, the next entry in the blockbuster video game franchise.

items.title: Drag x Drive review: The new chaotic game that is easy to pick up but incredibly difficult to master
items.summary: Drag x Drive wants to be Rocket League; one of my favourite games of all time. In concept, it nails the basics.

items.title: Briana went to an online meeting. Her team mate sent an AI assistant
items.summary: Recently, something odd happened to Briana Blackett. She was attending a meeting to give a colleague some content feedback, which isn't unusual in her volunteer work as a disability advocate.
